summary: P1 Enhancement: Story Quality Score in Comic Story Builder Step 2 - All features tested and verified working. Backend analyze-story endpoint returns AI-powered scores (not random). Frontend shows complete score card with 8 dimension bars, strengths, opportunities, and one-click improvement chips.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Story Quality Score feature fully verified. Backend /api/comic-storybook-v2/analyze-story returns real AI scores via gemini-3-flash-preview. Short prompts score < 50, detailed prompts score 70+. Frontend score card shows all 8 dimensions, strengths, opportunities, and quick-fix chips. Quick-fix chips call improve-idea API and update story text. Score card auto-clears when story is edited (stale score prevention). Dismiss and Re-analyze buttons work correctly.

details: Short prompt 'shy teenager magic' scored 38 (< 50 as expected)
Detailed prompt with protagonist/setting/conflict scored 82 (>= 70 as expected)
Too-short prompts (< 10 chars) rejected gracefully with success=false
Returns overall_score (0-100), dimensions (8 keys), strengths, opportunities, quick_fixes
Each quick_fix has label and instruction fields
Works across all genres (kids_adventure, superhero, fantasy, comedy, mystery)
Works across all age groups (3-6, 6-10, 8-12, 12+)
Button NOT visible when story idea < 10 characters
Button appears when story idea >= 10 characters
Button shows loading state during analysis
Button uses cyan color scheme (border-cyan-500/30)
Overall score displayed in circular badge (82 in test)
Band label shown ('Good, could be stronger')
8 dimension bars rendered with color coding (green/sky/amber/orange)
Dimension values shown (0-100 scale)
Strengths section with checkmark icons
Opportunities section with lightbulb icons
Quick fix chips rendered in purple color scheme
5 chips shown in test: Add stronger conflict, Define the hero, Add a magical element, Add a lesson
Clicking chip calls improve-idea API
Story text updated after chip click
Score card clears after chip click (stale score prevention)
Dismiss button visible in score card header
Clicking dismiss hides the score card
Re-analyze button visible at bottom of score card
Triggers new analysis when clicked
Improve My Idea button works independently of score card
Both features can be used together without conflict
